content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from Topic A can be repurposed to prioritize which new episodic memory entries an active learning agent commits to long-term memory, creating a self-supervised 'episodic memory pruning system that retains only high-curiosity unexpected experiences for future task transfer. This framework automatically prioritizes retaining out-of-distribution experiences that drive future learning, rather than discarding them based on manual thresholds.
